description: Find a Strongly Regular Graph with parameters v=99, k=14, λ=1, μ=2.

initialization_strategy.notes: Must guarantee the initial state is a valid simple 14-regular graph.

mutation_operator.name: degree_preserving_topological_swap
algorithm: 1. Uniformly sample two disjoint existing edges (u,v) and (x,y).
2. Verify that edges (u,x) and (v,y) do NOT currently exist.
3. If they exist, reject and resample.

energy_function.name: srg_algebraic_deviation
energy_function.description: Computes the Frobenius norm of the deviation from the SRG identity. Perfect SRG evaluates to E=0.
energy_function.notes: Because A is guaranteed 14-regular by the mutation operator, degree penalty terms are omitted. The diagonal inherently maps to 0.

properties: irreflexive: ∀ i, adj i i = false
symmetric: ∀ i j, adj i j = adj j i
λ=1: ∀ i j, adj i j → commonNeighbors adj i j = 1
μ=2: ∀ i j, ¬adj i j → i ≠ j → commonNeighbors adj i j = 2
